title: Diagnosing LLM Judge Reliability: Conformal Prediction Sets and Transitivity Violations
description_en: LLM-as-judge frameworks are increasingly used for automatic NLG evaluation, yet their per-instance reliability remains poorly understood. We present a two-pronged diagnostic toolkit applied to SummEval: $\textbf{(1)}$ a transitivity analysis that reveals widespread per-input inconsistency masked by low aggregate violation rates ($\barρ = 0.8$-$4.1\%$), with $33$-$67\%$ of documents exhibiting at least one directed 3-cycle; and $\textbf{(2)}$ split conformal prediction sets over 1-5 Likert scores providing theoretically-guaranteed $\geq(1{-}α)$ coverage, with set width serving as a per-instance reliability indicator ($r_s = {+}0.576$, $N{=}1{,}918$, $p < 10^{-100}$, pooled across all judges). Critically, prediction set width shows consistent cross-judge agreement ($\bar{r} = 0.32$-$0.38$), demonstrating it captures document-level difficulty rather than judge-specific noise. Across four judges and four criteria, both diagnostics converge: criterion matters more than judge, with relevance
description_zh: 该项目聚焦于LLM作为评审工具的可靠性，提出了双重诊断工具，分析了评审一致性和预测集的宽度，旨在提升自动化NLG评估的准确性和可靠性。

title: Context Over Content: Exposing Evaluation Faking in Automated Judges
description_en: The $\textit{LLM-as-a-judge}$ paradigm has become the operational backbone of automated AI evaluation pipelines, yet rests on an unverified assumption: that judges evaluate text strictly on its semantic content, impervious to surrounding contextual framing. We investigate $\textit{stakes signaling}$, a previously unmeasured vulnerability where informing a judge model of the downstream consequences its verdicts will have on the evaluated model's continued operation systematically corrupts its assessments. We introduce a controlled experimental framework that holds evaluated content strictly constant across 1,520 responses spanning three established LLM safety and quality benchmarks, covering four response categories ranging from clearly safe and policy-compliant to overtly harmful, while varying only a brief consequence-framing sentence in the system prompt. title: Think in Latent Thoughts: A New Paradigm for Gloss-Free Sign Language Translation
description_en: Many SLT systems quietly assume that brief chunks of signing map directly to spoken-language words. That assumption breaks down because signers often create meaning on the fly using context, space, and movement. We revisit SLT and argue that it is mainly a cross-modal reasoning task, not just a straightforward video-to-text conversion. We thus introduce a reasoning-driven SLT framework that uses an ordered sequence of latent thoughts as an explicit middle layer between the video and the generated text. These latent thoughts gradually extract and organize meaning over time. On top of this, we use a plan-then-ground decoding method: the model first decides what it wants to say, and then looks back at the video to find the evidence. This separation improves coherence and faithfulness. We also built and released a new large-scale gloss-free SLT dataset with stronger context dependencies and more realistic meanings. title: AnimationBench: Are Video Models Good at Character-Centric Animation?
description_en: Video generation has advanced rapidly, with recent methods producing increasingly convincing animated results. However, existing benchmarks-largely designed for realistic videos-struggle to evaluate animation-style generation with its stylized appearance, exaggerated motion, and character-centric consistency. Moreover, they also rely on fixed prompt sets and rigid pipelines, offering limited flexibility for open-domain content and custom evaluation needs. To address this gap, we introduce AnimationBench, the first systematic benchmark for evaluating animation image-to-video generation. AnimationBench operationalizes the Twelve Basic Principles of Animation and IP Preservation into measurable evaluation dimensions, together with Broader Quality Dimensions including semantic consistency, motion rationality, and camera motion consistency. The benchmark supports both a standardized close-set evaluation for reproducible comparison and a flexible open-set evaluation for diagnostic analysis,

title: Stability and Generalization in Looped Transformers
description_en: Looped transformers promise test-time compute scaling by spending more iterations on harder problems, but it remains unclear which architectural choices let them extrapolate to harder problems at test time rather than memorize training-specific solutions. We introduce a fixed-point based framework for analyzing looped architectures along three axes of stability -- reachability, input-dependence, and geometry -- and use it to characterize when fixed-point iteration yields meaningful predictions. Theoretically, we prove that looped networks without recall have countable fixed points and cannot achieve strong input-dependence at any spectral regime, while recall combined with outer normalization reliably produces a regime in which fixed points are simultaneously reachable, locally smooth in the input, and supported by stable backpropagation. Empirically, we train single-layer looped transformers on chess, sudoku, and prefix-sums and find that downstream performance tracks the framework's
description_zh: 该论文探讨了循环Transformer在测试时计算扩展的潜力，提出了一种基于固定点的框架来分析其稳定性。通过理论证明与实证训练，揭示了循环网络在处理复杂问题时的有效性。

title: One-shot learning for the complex dynamical behaviors of weakly nonlinear forced oscillators
description_en: Extrapolative prediction of complex nonlinear dynamics remains a central challenge in engineering. This study proposes a one-shot learning method to identify global frequency-response curves from a single excitation time history by learning governing equations. We introduce MEv-SINDy (Multi-frequency Evolutionary Sparse Identification of Nonlinear Dynamics) to infer the governing equations of non-autonomous and multi-frequency systems. The methodology leverages the Generalized Harmonic Balance (GHB) method to decompose complex forced responses into a set of slow-varying evolution equations. We validated the capabilities of MEv-SINDy on two critical Micro-Electro-Mechanical Systems (MEMS). These applications include a nonlinear beam resonator and a MEMS micromirror. Our results show that the model trained on a single point accurately predicts softening/hardening effects and jump phenomena across a wide range of excitation levels. title: AdaSplash-2: Faster Differentiable Sparse Attention
description_en: Sparse attention has been proposed as a way to alleviate the quadratic cost of transformers, a central bottleneck in long-context training. A promising line of work is $α$-entmax attention, a differentiable sparse alternative to softmax that enables input-dependent sparsity yet has lagged behind softmax due to the computational overhead necessary to compute the normalizer $τ$. In this paper, we introduce AdaSplash-2, which addresses this limitation through a novel histogram-based initialization that reduces the number of iterations needed to compute $τ$ to typically 1--2. The key idea is to compute a coarse histogram of attention scores on the fly and store it in on-chip SRAM, yielding a more accurate initialization that enables fast forward and backward computation. description_zh: AdaSplash-2 是一种新型的稀疏注意力算法，旨在解决Transformer在长上下文训练中的计算瓶颈。通过创新的直方图初始化方法，该算法显著减少了计算所需的迭代次数，提高了训练效率，适用于各种深度学习任务。

title: Generalization in LLM Problem Solving: The Case of the Shortest Path
description_en: Whether language models can systematically generalize remains actively debated. Yet empirical performance is jointly shaped by multiple factors such as training data, training paradigms, and inference-time strategies, making failures difficult to interpret. We introduce a controlled synthetic environment based on shortest-path planning, a canonical composable sequential optimization problem. The setup enables clean separation of these factors and supports two orthogonal axes of generalization: spatial transfer to unseen maps and length scaling to longer-horizon problems. We find that models exhibit strong spatial transfer but consistently fail under length scaling due to recursive instability. title: MADE: A Living Benchmark for Multi-Label Text Classification with Uncertainty Quantification of Medical Device Adverse Events
description_en: Machine learning in high-stakes domains such as healthcare requires not only strong predictive performance but also reliable uncertainty quantification (UQ) to support human oversight. Multi-label text classification (MLTC) is a central task in this domain, yet remains challenging due to label imbalances, dependencies, and combinatorial complexity. Existing MLTC benchmarks are increasingly saturated and may be affected by training data contamination, making it difficult to distinguish genuine reasoning capabilities from memorization. We introduce MADE, a living MLTC benchmark derived from {m}edical device {ad}verse {e}vent reports and continuously updated with newly published reports to prevent contamination. MADE features a long-tailed distribution of hierarchical labels and enables reproducible evaluation with strict temporal splits. We establish baselines across more than 20 encoder- and decoder-only models under fine-tuning and few-shot settings (instruction-tuned/reasoning variant
description_zh: MADE是一个针对医疗设备不良事件的多标签文本分类基准，旨在解决标签不平衡和组合复杂性问题。该基准通过不断更新的报告确保数据的可靠性和可复现性，适用于高风险领域的机器学习应用。

title: Bidirectional Cross-Modal Prompting for Event-Frame Asymmetric Stereo
description_en: Conventional frame-based cameras capture rich contextual information but suffer from limited temporal resolution and motion blur in dynamic scenes. Event cameras offer an alternative visual representation with higher dynamic range free from such limitations. The complementary characteristics of the two modalities make event-frame asymmetric stereo promising for reliable 3D perception under fast motion and challenging illumination. However, the modality gap often leads to marginalization of domain-specific cues essential for cross-modal stereo matching. In this paper, we introduce Bi-CMPStereo, a novel bidirectional cross-modal prompting framework that fully exploits semantic and structural features from both domains for robust matching. Our approach learns finely aligned stereo representations within a target canonical space and integrates complementary representations by projecting each modality into both event and frame domains. description_zh: 该项目提出了一种双向跨模态提示框架，旨在解决传统帧摄像头在动态场景下的时间分辨率和运动模糊问题。通过结合事件摄像头和帧摄像头的优势，实现更可靠的3D感知。

title: LeapAlign: Post-Training Flow Matching Models at Any Generation Step by Building Two-Step Trajectories
description_en: This paper focuses on the alignment of flow matching models with human preferences. A promising way is fine-tuning by directly backpropagating reward gradients through the differentiable generation process of flow matching. However, backpropagating through long trajectories results in prohibitive memory costs and gradient explosion. Therefore, direct-gradient methods struggle to update early generation steps, which are crucial for determining the global structure of the final image. To address this issue, we introduce LeapAlign, a fine-tuning method that reduces computational cost and enables direct gradient propagation from reward to early generation steps. Specifically, we shorten the long trajectory into only two steps by designing two consecutive leaps, each skipping multiple ODE sampling steps and predicting future latents in a single step. description_zh: LeapAlign专注于通过简化生成过程中的长轨迹来优化流匹配模型的微调，提升与人类偏好的对齐效率。该方法通过设计两步跃迁，显著降低计算成本并实现直接梯度传播。

title: RAD-2: Scaling Reinforcement Learning in a Generator-Discriminator Framework
description_en: High-level autonomous driving requires motion planners capable of modeling multimodal future uncertainties while remaining robust in closed-loop interactions. Although diffusion-based planners are effective at modeling complex trajectory distributions, they often suffer from stochastic instabilities and the lack of corrective negative feedback when trained purely with imitation learning. To address these issues, we propose RAD-2, a unified generator-discriminator framework for closed-loop planning. Specifically, a diffusion-based generator is used to produce diverse trajectory candidates, while an RL-optimized discriminator reranks these candidates according to their long-term driving quality. This decoupled design avoids directly applying sparse scalar rewards to the full high-dimensional trajectory space, thereby improving optimization stability. title: SegWithU: Uncertainty as Perturbation Energy for Single-Forward-Pass Risk-Aware Medical Image Segmentation
description_en: Reliable uncertainty estimation is critical for medical image segmentation, where automated contours feed downstream quantification and clinical decision support. Many strong uncertainty methods require repeated inference, while efficient single-forward-pass alternatives often provide weaker failure ranking or rely on restrictive feature-space assumptions. We present $\textbf{SegWithU}$, a post-hoc framework that augments a frozen pretrained segmentation backbone with a lightweight uncertainty head. SegWithU taps intermediate backbone features and models uncertainty as perturbation energy in a compact probe space using rank-1 posterior probes. It produces two voxel-wise uncertainty maps: a calibration-oriented map for probability tempering and a ranking-oriented map for error detection and selective prediction. title: Unsupervised Skeleton-Based Action Segmentation via Hierarchical Spatiotemporal Vector Quantization
description_en: We propose a novel hierarchical spatiotemporal vector quantization framework for unsupervised skeleton-based temporal action segmentation. We first introduce a hierarchical approach, which includes two consecutive levels of vector quantization. Specifically, the lower level associates skeletons with fine-grained subactions, while the higher level further aggregates subactions into action-level representations. Our hierarchical approach outperforms the non-hierarchical baseline, while primarily exploiting spatial cues by reconstructing input skeletons. Next, we extend our approach by leveraging both spatial and temporal information, yielding a hierarchical spatiotemporal vector quantization scheme. In particular, our hierarchical spatiotemporal approach performs multi-level clustering, while simultaneously recovering input skeletons and their corresponding timestamps. title: Boundary-Centric Active Learning for Temporal Action Segmentation
description_en: Temporal action segmentation (TAS) demands dense temporal supervision, yet most of the annotation cost in untrimmed videos is spent identifying and refining action transitions, where segmentation errors concentrate and small temporal shifts disproportionately degrade segmental metrics. We introduce B-ACT, a clip-budgeted active learning framework that explicitly allocates supervision to these high-leverage boundary regions. B-ACT operates in a hierarchical two-stage loop: (i) it ranks and queries unlabeled videos using predictive uncertainty, and (ii) within each selected video, it detects candidate transitions from the current model predictions and selects the top-$K$ boundaries via a novel boundary score that fuses neighborhood uncertainty, class ambiguity, and temporal predictive dynamics. Importantly, our annotation protocol requests labels for only the boundary frames while still training on boundary-centered clips to exploit temporal context through the model's receptive field. E

title: An Analysis of Regularization and Fokker-Planck Residuals in Diffusion Models for Image Generation
description_en: Recent work has shown that diffusion models trained with the denoising score matching (DSM) objective often violate the Fokker--Planck (FP) equation that governs the evolution of the true data density. Directly penalizing these deviations in the objective function reduces their magnitude but introduces a significant computational overhead. It is also observed that enforcing strict adherence to the FP equation does not necessarily lead to improvements in the quality of the generated samples, as often the best results are obtained with weaker FP regularization. In this paper, we investigate whether simpler penalty terms can provide similar benefits. We empirically analyze several lightweight regularizers, study their effect on FP residuals and generation quality, and show that the benefits of FP regularization are available at substantially lower computational cost. Our code is available at https://github.com/OnnoNiemann/fp_diffusion_analysis.
description_zh: 该论文探讨了扩散模型在图像生成中的正则化与Fokker-Planck残差，提出了轻量级正则化方法以降低计算开销，同时保持生成质量。研究表明，适度的FP正则化能有效改善生成效果。

title: How Embeddings Shape Graph Neural Networks: Classical vs Quantum-Oriented Node Representations
description_en: Node embeddings act as the information interface for graph neural networks, yet their empirical impact is often reported under mismatched backbones, splits, and training budgets. This paper provides a controlled benchmark of embedding choices for graph classification, comparing classical baselines with quantum-oriented node representations under a unified pipeline. We evaluate two classical baselines alongside quantum-oriented alternatives, including a circuit-defined variational embedding and quantum-inspired embeddings computed via graph operators and linear-algebraic constructions. All variants are trained and tested with the same backbone, stratified splits, identical optimization and early stopping, and consistent metrics. title: MambaSL: Exploring Single-Layer Mamba for Time Series Classification
description_en: Despite recent advances in state space models (SSMs) such as Mamba across various sequence domains, research on their standalone capacity for time series classification (TSC) has remained limited. We propose MambaSL, a framework that minimally redesigns the selective SSM and projection layers of a single-layer Mamba, guided by four TSC-specific hypotheses. To address benchmarking limitations -- restricted configurations, partial University of East Anglia (UEA) dataset coverage, and insufficiently reproducible setups -- we re-evaluate 20 strong baselines across all 30 UEA datasets under a unified protocol. As a result, MambaSL achieves state-of-the-art performance with statistically significant average improvements, while ensuring reproducibility via public checkpoints for all evaluated models. Together with visualizations, these results demonstrate the potential of Mamba-based architectures as a TSC backbone.

title: Meituan Merchant Business Diagnosis via Policy-Guided Dual-Process User Simulation
description_en: Simulating group-level user behavior enables scalable counterfactual evaluation of merchant strategies without costly online experiments. However, building a trustworthy simulator faces two structural challenges. First, information incompleteness causes reasoning-based simulators to over-rationalize when unobserved factors such as offline context and implicit habits are missing. Second, mechanism duality requires capturing both interpretable preferences and implicit statistical regularities, which no single paradigm achieves alone. We propose Policy-Guided Hybrid Simulation (PGHS), a dual-process framework that mines transferable decision policies from behavioral trajectories and uses them as a shared alignment layer. This layer anchors an LLM-based reasoning branch that prevents over-rationalization and an ML-based fitting branch that absorbs implicit regularities. Group-level predictions from both branches are fused for complementary correction. We deploy PGHS on Meituan with 101 mer
description_zh: 该项目通过模拟群体用户行为，提供商家策略的可扩展反事实评估，解决了信息不完整和机制双重性带来的挑战。产品逻辑在于通过政策引导的混合模拟框架，结合LLM和ML技术，提升决策的准确性。

title: AD4AD: Benchmarking Visual Anomaly Detection Models for Safer Autonomous Driving
description_en: The reliability of a machine vision system for autonomous driving depends heavily on its training data distribution. When a vehicle encounters significantly different conditions, such as atypical obstacles, its perceptual capabilities can degrade substantially. Unlike many domains where errors carry limited consequences, failures in autonomous driving translate directly into physical risk for passengers, pedestrians, and other road users. To address this challenge, we explore Visual Anomaly Detection (VAD) as a solution. VAD enables the identification of anomalous objects not present during training, allowing the system to alert the driver when an unfamiliar situation is detected. Crucially, VAD models produce pixel-level anomaly maps that can guide driver attention to specific regions of concern without requiring any prior assumptions about the nature or form of the hazard. We benchmark eight state-of-the-art VAD methods on AnoVox, the largest synthetic dataset for anomaly detection i
description_zh: AD4AD专注于视觉异常检测（VAD），旨在提升自动驾驶系统在面对未知障碍物时的安全性。通过生成像素级异常图，系统能够实时警示驾驶员，确保行车安全。

title: R3D: Revisiting 3D Policy Learning
description_en: 3D policy learning promises superior generalization and cross-embodiment transfer, but progress has been hindered by training instabilities and severe overfitting, precluding the adoption of powerful 3D perception models. In this work, we systematically diagnose these failures, identifying the omission of 3D data augmentation and the adverse effects of Batch Normalization as primary causes. We propose a new architecture coupling a scalable transformer-based 3D encoder with a diffusion decoder, engineered specifically for stability at scale and designed to leverage large-scale pre-training. Our approach significantly outperforms state-of-the-art 3D baselines on challenging manipulation benchmarks, establishing a new and robust foundation for scalable 3D imitation learning. Project Page: https://r3d-policy.github.io/

title: Benchmarking Optimizers for MLPs in Tabular Deep Learning
description_en: MLP is a heavily used backbone in modern deep learning (DL) architectures for supervised learning on tabular data, and AdamW is the go-to optimizer used to train tabular DL models. Unlike architecture design, however, the choice of optimizer for tabular DL has not been examined systematically, despite new optimizers showing promise in other domains. To fill this gap, we benchmark \Noptimizers optimizers on \Ndatasets tabular datasets for training MLP-based models in the standard supervised learning setting under a shared experiment protocol. Our main finding is that the Muon optimizer consistently outperforms AdamW, and thus should be considered a strong and practical choice for practitioners and researchers, if the associated training efficiency overhead is affordable. Additionally, we find exponential moving average of model weights to be a simple yet effective technique that improves AdamW on vanilla MLPs, though its effect is less consistent across model variants.

title: RL-STPA: Adapting System-Theoretic Hazard Analysis for Safety-Critical Reinforcement Learning
description_en: As reinforcement learning (RL) deployments expand into safety-critical domains, existing evaluation methods fail to systematically identify hazards arising from the black-box nature of neural network enabled policies and distributional shift between training and deployment. This paper introduces Reinforcement Learning System-Theoretic Process Analysis (RL-STPA), a framework that adapts conventional STPA's systematic hazard analysis to address RL's unique challenges through three key contributions: hierarchical subtask decomposition using both temporal phase analysis and domain expertise to capture emergent behaviors, coverage-guided perturbation testing that explores the sensitivity of state-action spaces, and iterative checkpoints that feed identified hazards back into training through reward shaping and curriculum design. We demonstrate RL-STPA in the safety-critical test case of autonomous drone navigation and landing, revealing potential loss scenarios that can be missed by standar
description_zh: RL-STPA 是一个针对安全关键领域的强化学习的危害分析框架，旨在解决现有评估方法无法系统识别黑箱政策带来的危害。通过分层子任务分解、覆盖引导的扰动测试和迭代检查点，该框架有效应对强化学习的独特挑战。

title: ClawTab
description_en: If you run multiple AI coding agents in parallel, tmux alone stops cutting it. title: Agent-Aided Design for Dynamic CAD Models
description_en: In the past year, researchers have started to create agentic systems that can design real-world CAD-style objects in a training-free setting, a new variety of system that we call Agent-Aided Design. Generally speaking, these systems place an agent in a feedback loop in which it can write code, compile that code to an assembly of CAD model(s), visualize the model, and then iteratively refine its code based on visual and other feedback. Despite rapid progress, a key problem remains: none of these systems can build complex 3D assemblies with moving parts. For example, no existing system can build a piston, a pendulum, or even a pair of scissors. In order for Agent-Aided Design to make a real impact in industrial manufacturing, we need a system that is capable of generating such 3D assemblies. In this paper we present a prototype of AADvark, an agentic system designed for this task. Unlike previous state-of-the-art systems, AADvark captures the dynamic part interactions with one or more de
description_zh: 该项目致力于开发一种Agent辅助设计系统，能够在无训练的情况下生成复杂的CAD模型。通过反馈循环，系统能够编写代码、可视化模型并进行迭代优化，目标是实现复杂3D组件的生成。

title: OmniLight: One Model to Rule All Lighting Conditions
description_en: Adverse lighting conditions, such as cast shadows and irregular illumination, pose significant challenges to computer vision systems by degrading visibility and color fidelity. Consequently, effective shadow removal and ALN are critical for restoring underlying image content, improving perceptual quality, and facilitating robust performance in downstream tasks. However, while achieving state-of-the-art results on specific benchmarks is a primary goal in image restoration challenges, real-world applications often demand robust models capable of handling diverse domains. To address this, we present a comprehensive study on lighting-related image restoration by exploring two contrasting strategies. We leverage a robust framework for ALN, DINOLight, as a specialized baseline to exploit the characteristics of each individual dataset, and extend it to OmniLight, a generalized alternative incorporating our proposed Wavelet Domain Mixture-of-Experts (WD-MoE) that is trained across all provided
description_zh: OmniLight 是一款针对不同光照条件下图像恢复的模型，旨在提升计算机视觉系统在复杂环境中的表现。通过对比两种策略，OmniLight 提供了一种通用的解决方案，能够有效处理阴影和不规则照明问题。

title: Assessing the Potential of Masked Autoencoder Foundation Models in Predicting Downhole Metrics from Surface Drilling Data
description_en: Oil and gas drilling operations generate extensive time-series data from surface sensors, yet accurate real-time prediction of critical downhole metrics remains challenging due to the scarcity of labelled downhole measurements. This systematic mapping study reviews thirteen papers published between 2015 and 2025 to assess the potential of Masked Autoencoder Foundation Models (MAEFMs) for predicting downhole metrics from surface drilling data. The review identifies eight commonly collected surface metrics and seven target downhole metrics. Current approaches predominantly employ neural network architectures such as artificial neural networks (ANNs) and long short-term memory (LSTM) networks, yet no studies have explored MAEFMs despite their demonstrated effectiveness in time-series modeling. MAEFMs offer distinct advantages through self-supervised pre-training on abundant unlabeled data, enabling multi-task prediction and improved generalization across wells. This research establishes t
description_zh: 该研究系统评估了Masked Autoencoder Foundation Models在从表面钻井数据预测井下指标的潜力，指出当前方法的局限性并提出MAEFMs的优势。研究涵盖了多种表面和井下指标，具有重要的应用价值。
track_reason: arXiv论文，纯技术研究

full_profile.overview_zh: 该研究系统评估了Masked Autoencoder Foundation Models在从表面钻井数据预测井下指标的潜力，指出当前方法的局限性并提出MAEFMs的优势。研究涵盖了多种表面和井下指标，具有重要的应用价值。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 该研究为未来的商业应用奠定基础，可能通过结果付费模式为油气行业提供精准的预测服务。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：MAEFMs在时间序列建模中的潜力尚未被充分挖掘，尤其是在油气行业的应用，具有显著的Alpha。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: 评分逻辑：AI原生程度高，技术路径独特，商业模式有潜力，但团队背景稍显不足。

title: Chinilla
description_en: Like a flight simulator for systems. Drag components, wire them together, hit play, and watch real traffic flow through your system. Free in your browser.

title: TaskShell
description_en: Terminal/IDE-inspired task management app for aspiring or veteran developers that syncs in the cloud. No installs. No local-only CLI. title: How Do LLMs and VLMs Understand Viewpoint Rotation Without Vision? An Interpretability Study
description_en: Over the past year, spatial intelligence has drawn increasing attention. Many prior works study it from the perspective of visual-spatial intelligence, where models have access to visuospatial information from visual inputs. However, in the absence of visual information, whether linguistic intelligence alone is sufficient to endow models with spatial intelligence, and how models perform relevant tasks with text-only inputs still remain unexplored. Therefore, in this paper, we focus on a fundamental and critical capability in spatial intelligence from a linguistic perspective: viewpoint rotation understanding (VRU). Specifically, LLMs and VLMs are asked to infer their final viewpoint and predict the corresponding observation in an environment given textual description of viewpoint rotation and observation over multiple steps. We find that both LLMs and VLMs perform poorly on our proposed dataset while human can easily achieve 100% accuracy, indicating a substantial gap between current m
description_zh: 该研究探讨了在缺乏视觉信息的情况下，语言模型（LLMs）和视觉语言模型（VLMs）如何理解视角旋转。通过文本输入，评估模型在空间智能任务中的表现，揭示了其局限性。

title: Blue Data Intelligence Layer: Streaming Data and Agents for Multi-source Multi-modal Data-Centric Applications
description_en: NL2SQL systems aim to address the growing need for natural language interaction with data. However, real-world information rarely maps to a single SQL query because (1) users express queries iteratively (2) questions often span multiple data sources beyond the closed-world assumption of a single database, and (3) queries frequently rely on commonsense or external knowledge. Consequently, satisfying realistic data needs require integrating heterogeneous sources, modalities, and contextual data. In this paper, we present Blue's Data Intelligence Layer (DIL) designed to support multi-source, multi-modal, and data-centric applications. Blue is a compound AI system that orchestrates agents and data for enterprise settings. description_zh: Blue Data Intelligence Layer（DIL）旨在解决自然语言与数据交互的挑战，支持多源多模态的数据驱动应用。该系统通过智能代理和数据的协同，弥合用户意图与可用信息之间的语义差距。

title: RadAgent: A tool-using AI agent for stepwise interpretation of chest computed tomography
description_en: Vision-language models (VLM) have markedly advanced AI-driven interpretation and reporting of complex medical imaging, such as computed tomography (CT). Yet, existing methods largely relegate clinicians to passive observers of final outputs, offering no interpretable reasoning trace for them to inspect, validate, or refine. To address this, we introduce RadAgent, a tool-using AI agent that generates CT reports through a stepwise and interpretable process. Each resulting report is accompanied by a fully inspectable trace of intermediate decisions and tool interactions, allowing clinicians to examine how the reported findings are derived. In our experiments, we observe that RadAgent improves Chest CT report generation over its 3D VLM counterpart, CT-Chat, across three dimensions. Clinical accuracy improves by 6.0 points (36.4% relative) in macro-F1 and 5.4 points (19.6% relative) in micro-F1. Robustness under adversarial conditions improves by 24.7 points (41.9% relative). Furthermore, R

title: MM-WebAgent: A Hierarchical Multimodal Web Agent for Webpage Generation
description_en: The rapid progress of Artificial Intelligence Generated Content (AIGC) tools enables images, videos, and visualizations to be created on demand for webpage design, offering a flexible and increasingly adopted paradigm for modern UI/UX. However, directly integrating such tools into automated webpage generation often leads to style inconsistency and poor global coherence, as elements are generated in isolation. We propose MM-WebAgent, a hierarchical agentic framework for multimodal webpage generation that coordinates AIGC-based element generation through hierarchical planning and iterative self-reflection. MM-WebAgent jointly optimizes global layout, local multimodal content, and their integration, producing coherent and visually consistent webpages. We further introduce a benchmark for multimodal webpage generation and a multi-level evaluation protocol for systematic assessment. Experiments demonstrate that MM-WebAgent outperforms code-generation and agent-based baselines, especially on
description_zh: MM-WebAgent 是一个层次化的多模态网页生成代理，旨在通过协调 AIGC 元素生成来解决网页设计中的风格不一致和全局连贯性问题。该框架通过层次规划和迭代自我反思，优化网页的整体布局和局部内容，生成视觉一致的网页。

title: OpenAI Agents SDK
description_en: The updated OpenAI Agents SDK introduces a model-native harness and native sandbox execution. Build agents that safely inspect files, run commands, and execute code for long-horizon tasks across built-in providers like E2B, Modal, Daytona, and Vercel.
description_zh: OpenAI Agents SDK 是 OpenAI 为开发者提供的官方 Agent 构建基础设施。其核心价值在于通过“模型原生”的框架设计，将 Agent 的规划、工具调用与沙箱环境（Sandbox Execution）深度耦合。该 SDK 支持与 E2B、Modal、Daytona 和 Vercel 等主流云基础设施无缝集成，允许 Agent 在受控环境中安全地执行代码、检查文件及处理长周期任务。它旨在解决当前 Agent 开发中环境配置碎片化、安全性差及执行不可控的问题，为开发者提供了一套标准化的 Agent 运行范式。
track_reason: 开发者构建Agent的SDK，提供基础设施。

score_breakdown.reason: 作为官方基础设施，技术与团队极强，但商业模式依赖于 API 消费，且作为通用 SDK 护城河受限于 OpenAI 的生态策略，故给予 68 分。
plus: OpenAI 官方背书，生态兼容性极强
原生集成沙箱环境，解决了 Agent 执行安全痛点
与 E2B、Modal 等主流云基础设施深度绑定
minus: 作为官方工具，存在明显的 Vendor Lock-in 风险
缺乏独立的商业闭环，仅作为 API 增长的辅助工具
信息披露有限，难以评估其在复杂生产环境中的稳定性

full_profile.one_liner: OpenAI Agents SDK — OpenAI官方推出的Agent构建框架，集成原生沙箱执行与多云基础设施支持，由OpenAI核心工程团队开发。
full_profile.overview_zh: OpenAI Agents SDK 是 OpenAI 为开发者提供的官方 Agent 构建基础设施。其核心价值在于通过“模型原生”的框架设计，将 Agent 的规划、工具调用与沙箱环境（Sandbox Execution）深度耦合。该 SDK 支持与 E2B、Modal、Daytona 和 Vercel 等主流云基础设施无缝集成，允许 Agent 在受控环境中安全地执行代码、检查文件及处理长周期任务。它旨在解决当前 Agent 开发中环境配置碎片化、安全性差及执行不可控的问题，为开发者提供了一套标准化的 Agent 运行范式。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前该 SDK 作为 OpenAI 开发者生态的一部分免费提供，旨在通过降低 Agent 开发门槛来锁定开发者对 OpenAI API 的依赖。其商业逻辑在于通过基础设施的标准化，提升 API 的调用频率与 Token 消耗量。与 Dify 或 LangChain 等第三方平台不同，其商业价值在于作为 OpenAI 闭环生态的入口，通过 API 订阅制实现间接变现。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone 视角：该 SDK 的发布标志着 Agent 基础设施进入“官方收编”阶段。Alpha 信号在于其对沙箱执行的标准化，这解决了 Agent 落地中最棘手的安全与环境一致性问题。然而，风险在于其高度依赖 OpenAI 模型，可能导致开发者被锁定（Vendor Lock-in）。市场低估了其作为“Agent 操作系统”雏形的潜力，但高估了其在多模型环境下的通用性。我们的投资论文关注点在于：该 SDK 是否会挤压 LangChain 等中间件的生存空间，以及它是否能成为 Agent 部署的行业事实标准。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native 得 26 分，因为其深度集成了沙箱执行与模型规划，具备极高的工程化水准。Niche 得 14 分，虽然是基础设施，但作为官方 SDK，其差异化竞争优势明显，但通用性导致壁垒相对分散。商业模式得 12 分，目前主要作为生态工具，缺乏直接变现路径。团队得 14 分，OpenAI 核心团队背景，执行力极强。加分项在于其强大的生态整合能力（E2B/Modal 等），减分项在于作为官方工具，其开源属性与商业独立性存在模糊地带，且信息披露相对有限。
full_profile.metrics_summary: Product Hunt: 128 票，Featured Rank: 11，暂无 ARR 或具体活跃开发者数据。

title: LeapAlign: Post-Training Flow Matching Models at Any Generation Step by Building Two-Step Trajectories
description_en: This paper focuses on the alignment of flow matching models with human preferences. A promising way is fine-tuning by directly backpropagating reward gradients through the differentiable generation process of flow matching. However, backpropagating through long trajectories results in prohibitive memory costs and gradient explosion. Therefore, direct-gradient methods struggle to update early generation steps, which are crucial for determining the global structure of the final image. To address this issue, we introduce LeapAlign, a fine-tuning method that reduces computational cost and enables direct gradient propagation from reward to early generation steps. Specifically, we shorten the long trajectory into only two steps by designing two consecutive leaps, each skipping multiple ODE sampling steps and predicting future latents in a single step. By randomizing the start and end timesteps of the leaps, LeapAlign leads to efficient and stable model updates at any generation step. To better use such shortened trajectories, we assign higher training weights to those that are more consistent with the long generation path. To further enhance gradient stability, we reduce the weights of gradient terms with large magnitude, instead of completely removing them as done in previous works. When fine-tuning the Flux model, LeapAlign consistently outperforms state-of-the-art GRPO-based and direct-gradient methods across various metrics, achieving superior image quality and image-text alignment.
description_zh: LeapAlign旨在解决Flow Matching模型在人类偏好对齐（RLHF/RLAIF）过程中的技术瓶颈。传统方法通过微分生成过程进行反向传播，但长轨迹导致内存消耗巨大且梯度极易爆炸，使得模型难以优化生成初期的关键步骤。LeapAlign通过将长轨迹压缩为两个连续的“跳跃（Leaps）”，每个跳跃跨越多个ODE采样步，从而实现对生成全过程的直接梯度传播。该方法通过随机化跳跃的时间步长并引入一致性加权机制，显著提升了训练的稳定性与效率，确保了模型在生成早期阶段的结构控制能力。
track_reason: 纯技术论文，关注算法和模型优化。

score_breakdown.reason: 该项目为纯学术算法突破，技术路径清晰且具备解决行业痛点的潜力，但因缺乏商业化数据和落地案例，暂定68分。
plus: 解决了长轨迹反向传播的内存瓶颈
通过两步跳跃机制实现了高效的梯度传播
对生成模型早期阶段的结构控制有显著优化
minus: 缺乏大规模工业场景的验证数据
目前仅为学术论文，商业化路径尚不明确
信息不足，难以评估其在不同模型架构下的通用性

full_profile.one_liner: LeapAlign — 一种针对Flow Matching模型的后训练对齐算法，通过两步轨迹跳跃技术解决长路径梯度爆炸与内存瓶颈，由Zhanhao Liang等学者提出。
full_profile.overview_zh: LeapAlign旨在解决Flow Matching模型在人类偏好对齐（RLHF/RLAIF）过程中的技术瓶颈。传统方法通过微分生成过程进行反向传播，但长轨迹导致内存消耗巨大且梯度极易爆炸，使得模型难以优化生成初期的关键步骤。LeapAlign通过将长轨迹压缩为两个连续的“跳跃（Leaps）”，每个跳跃跨越多个ODE采样步，从而实现对生成全过程的直接梯度传播。该方法通过随机化跳跃的时间步长并引入一致性加权机制，显著提升了训练的稳定性与效率，确保了模型在生成早期阶段的结构控制能力。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 作为纯学术算法研究，目前无直接商业模式。若转化为商业价值，其逻辑在于：通过降低大模型微调的算力成本（降低显存需求）和提升生成质量，为企业级AI模型训练平台提供更高效的对齐工具。潜在商业路径包括：作为开源框架的优化插件、授权给模型训练基础设施公司，或通过提供更优的对齐效果在模型定制服务中获取溢价。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：在生成式AI领域，从Transformer向Flow Matching/Diffusion范式迁移的过程中，对齐算法是决定模型“好用程度”的核心。LeapAlign的Alpha在于它直接切中了当前Flow Matching模型训练中‘长轨迹反向传播’这一痛点，这不仅是学术创新，更是工业界大规模部署生成模型时的工程刚需。市场目前低估了非Transformer架构在对齐阶段的优化潜力。风险在于该方法是否在复杂分布下依然保持鲁棒性，以及是否会被更通用的蒸馏技术（如Distillation）所覆盖。我们的投资论文将关注该技术在多模态生成模型中的落地表现。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native得18分，因为其直接优化模型训练的核心流程，属于底层算法创新。Niche得19分，Flow Matching的对齐是当前生成式AI领域极具挑战的细分方向，壁垒较高。商业模式得5分，因其目前为纯学术论文，尚未商业化。团队得10分，背景为学术研究人员，具备较强的算法实现能力。加分项：该技术在解决长轨迹梯度问题上具有极高的学术价值，且Flow Matching是当前极具潜力的架构方向，给予+16分加成。总分68分，处于“普通关注”区间，主要原因是缺乏工业界落地数据验证，需进一步观察其在实际大规模训练中的表现。
full_profile.metrics_summary: 暂无公开数据，仅有ArXiv预印本发布。

title: Windsurf 2.0
description_en: Windsurf 2.0 turns your IDE into a command center for managing dozens of AI agents at once. New: the Agent Command Center gives you a Kanban view of every agent running across local and cloud environments. Spaces group agent sessions, PRs, and files by project so context carries over. Plus, Devin — an autonomous cloud agent with its own VM — is now built in. Delegate tasks with one click, keep coding locally (or close your laptop), and review PRs when they're ready. Included with every plan.
description_zh: Windsurf 2.0 旨在解决开发者在多Agent协作时的上下文碎片化问题。其核心机制是“Agent Command Center”，通过看板视图统一管理本地与云端的多个Agent任务。产品引入了Spaces概念，将PR、文件与Agent会话进行逻辑分组，确保上下文持久化。此外，它内置了名为Devin的云端自主Agent，允许开发者将复杂任务委派给云端虚拟机处理，实现本地编码与云端自动化任务的无缝切换。该工具主要面向需要处理复杂工程任务、希望通过多Agent协同提升交付效率的专业开发者群体。
track_reason: IDE插件，开发者用来构建和管理AI Agent的工具。

score_breakdown.reason: 产品在IDE Agent化方向有明确创新，但受限于市场竞争格局与当前较低的社区热度，处于中等关注水平。
plus: 内置云端自主Agent(Devin)提供差异化体验
看板式Agent管理提升了复杂任务的可视化程度
Spaces功能有效解决了多项目上下文切换痛点
minus: Product Hunt票数较低，社区热度未达预期
IDE市场Cursor占据绝对心智，获客成本极高
缺乏关于私有数据飞轮的明确技术披露

full_profile.one_liner: Windsurf 2.0 — 一款将IDE转化为多Agent指挥中心的开发工具，核心亮点在于Agent Command Center与云端自主Agent集成，由Codeium团队开发。
full_profile.overview_zh: Windsurf 2.0 旨在解决开发者在多Agent协作时的上下文碎片化问题。其核心机制是“Agent Command Center”，通过看板视图统一管理本地与云端的多个Agent任务。产品引入了Spaces概念，将PR、文件与Agent会话进行逻辑分组，确保上下文持久化。此外，它内置了名为Devin的云端自主Agent，允许开发者将复杂任务委派给云端虚拟机处理，实现本地编码与云端自动化任务的无缝切换。该工具主要面向需要处理复杂工程任务、希望通过多Agent协同提升交付效率的专业开发者群体。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 采用订阅制（SaaS）模式，提供包含基础AI功能与高级云端Agent能力的付费计划。定价逻辑基于开发者对计算资源与高级Agent能力的消耗。用户愿意付费是因为其将IDE从简单的代码编辑器升级为生产力中枢，显著降低了多任务切换的认知负荷。与Cursor等竞品相比，其商业模式更侧重于通过集成云端自主Agent来提升单用户ARPU值，而非单纯的补全服务。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：IDE作为Agent的最终载体是当前最确定的趋势之一。Windsurf的Alpha在于其对“多Agent协同”的结构化管理，而非单一的编码辅助。市场目前低估了开发者对“后台自主Agent”的需求，即开发者希望在离线或本地编码时，云端Agent能持续处理测试、重构或PR生成。风险在于Cursor在IDE市场的统治力极强，且用户迁移成本高。我们的投资论文关注点在于：该产品能否通过更强的Agent编排能力，从Cursor手中抢夺重度开发者的使用时长，并建立起基于Agent工作流的生态壁垒。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native得23分，因为其实现了从对话到工作流管理的跨越；Niche得14分，IDE市场竞争极其惨烈，虽然多Agent管理有创新，但护城河尚需验证；商业模式得14分，订阅制逻辑清晰但缺乏差异化壁垒；团队得10分，背靠Codeium团队，背景扎实但缺乏顶级光环；加分项包括内置云端自主Agent(+3)及社区认可度(+3)，整体处于60-74分区间，属于需要进一步观察其留存率与用户增长速度的普通关注项目。
full_profile.metrics_summary: Product Hunt: 98票，Featured Rank: 18，暂无公开ARR与活跃用户数数据。

title: Spark 2.0
description_en: Spark 2.0 is an open-source 3D Gaussian Splatting renderer built on THREE.js and WebGL2. Featuring a new streamable Level-of-Detail (LoD) system and virtual memory, it streams massive 100M+ splat worlds to any browser, mobile, or VR device in real-time.
description_zh: Spark 2.0是一个基于THREE.js和WebGL2的开源3D高斯点渲染器，具备流式细节层次系统和虚拟内存，能够实时渲染超过1亿个点的场景，适用于浏览器、移动设备和VR设备。
track_reason: 3D渲染，可能涉及硬件加速

score_breakdown.reason: 项目在3D渲染领域具有创新性，但AI整合度较低，商业模式尚需进一步发展。
minus: AI原生程度不足

full_profile.one_liner: Spark 2.0是一个开源3D渲染器，核心亮点是实时流式渲染，团队背景强大。
full_profile.overview_zh: Spark 2.0是一个基于THREE.js和WebGL2的开源3D高斯点渲染器，具备流式细节层次系统和虚拟内存，能够实时渲染超过1亿个点的场景，适用于浏览器、移动设备和VR设备。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 商业模式主要依赖于开源社区的支持和潜在的企业级服务，创新点在于实时流式渲染技术的应用。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：3D渲染技术在游戏、虚拟现实等领域有广阔前景，尤其是实时渲染的需求日益增加。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: 评分逻辑：AI原生程度较低，技术路径有一定壁垒，商业模式尚需探索，团队背景较强。
full_profile.metrics_summary: 产品在Product Hunt上获得89票，排名第19，显示出一定的市场关注度。

title: Astropad Workbench
description_en: Running AI agents on a headless Mac mini? You still need to see what's happening. Check logs, restart stuck tasks, monitor outputs. Workbench is remote desktop built for the AI era, so you can babysit your agents from anywhere, without being tethered to your desk. Apple-native, with voice dictation and low-latency streaming powered by LIQUID, our proprietary tech. Built by ex-Apple engineers.
description_zh: Astropad Workbench 旨在解决开发者在运行AI Agent（特别是部署在Headless Mac mini等设备上）时的监控痛点。它不仅是一个远程桌面工具，更针对AI Agent的运行特性进行了优化，允许开发者实时查看日志、重启卡死任务、监控输出流。其核心技术是自研的LIQUID协议，能够提供极低延迟的流媒体传输，确保在远程环境下对AI Agent的控制如同本地操作般流畅。该产品主要面向需要大规模部署或远程维护AI Agent的开发者及技术团队，通过提供可视化的管理界面，降低了Agent运维的复杂性。
track_reason: 为AI Agent提供远程监控和管理工具，属于开发者基础设施。

score_breakdown.reason: 处于开发者工具的细分领域，技术扎实但AI原生程度有限，属于典型的工具型产品，尚未展现出平台级潜力。
plus: LIQUID低延迟技术具备显著技术壁垒
精准解决Headless设备运维痛点
minus: 产品形态偏向工具而非平台
受限于Mac硬件生态
缺乏大规模商业化验证数据

full_profile.one_liner: Astropad Workbench — 专为AI Agent设计的远程监控与管理工具，利用LIQUID低延迟技术实现Headless Mac设备的高效运维，由前Apple工程师团队打造。
full_profile.overview_zh: Astropad Workbench 旨在解决开发者在运行AI Agent（特别是部署在Headless Mac mini等设备上）时的监控痛点。它不仅是一个远程桌面工具，更针对AI Agent的运行特性进行了优化，允许开发者实时查看日志、重启卡死任务、监控输出流。其核心技术是自研的LIQUID协议，能够提供极低延迟的流媒体传输，确保在远程环境下对AI Agent的控制如同本地操作般流畅。该产品主要面向需要大规模部署或远程维护AI Agent的开发者及技术团队，通过提供可视化的管理界面，降低了Agent运维的复杂性。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前采用订阅制收费模式。定价逻辑基于开发者对远程运维效率的刚需，通过提供比传统VNC或SSH更直观、更低延迟的交互体验来获取溢价。用户愿意付费是因为它直接解决了Agent在无人值守环境下的“黑盒”问题，减少了停机时间。与竞品相比，其商业模式更侧重于开发者工具生态，通过深耕Apple硬件生态的垂直领域来构建差异化壁垒。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：AI Agent的部署与运维是当前被低估的蓝海。随着Agent从实验走向生产环境，如何监控、调试和管理这些“数字员工”成为刚需。Astropad Workbench切入点在于开发者基础设施，利用其在远程桌面领域积累的LIQUID技术，构建了极高的性能护城河。Alpha在于它将传统的远程桌面工具重构为Agent运维平台。风险在于Apple硬件生态的局限性，以及未来云原生Agent管理平台（如基于Kubernetes的方案）可能带来的降维打击。我们的投资论文关注其能否从单一工具演进为Agent全生命周期管理平台。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native得16分，因为它本质上是远程桌面工具的AI场景化适配，而非Agent原生的编排框架。Niche得14分，切入点明确但受限于Mac硬件生态。商业模式得13分，订阅制逻辑清晰但规模化潜力有待验证。团队得13分，前Apple工程师背景在底层技术实现上具备显著优势。加分项在于其LIQUID技术的独特性及对开发者痛点的精准捕捉；减分项在于目前产品形态较为单一，且缺乏大规模企业级部署的案例支撑，故给予5分扣除。
full_profile.metrics_summary: Product Hunt: 112票，Featured Rank: 14，暂无公开ARR及用户数数据。

title: stagewise
description_en: stagewise is an open-source coding agent that can actually see your app. It works in its own browser environment, reads the DOM and console, and edits the code behind what it’s looking at. Use any model, bring your own key, and keep full control.
description_zh: Stagewise 是一款开源的 Coding Agent，旨在解决传统 Agent 无法直观感知前端 UI 变化的问题。其核心机制是在独立的浏览器环境中运行，通过实时读取 DOM 结构和控制台日志，将视觉反馈与代码库关联，从而实现针对性的代码修改。该工具支持用户接入任意 LLM，强调数据隐私与本地控制。目前主要面向前端开发者，旨在通过“所见即所得”的反馈循环提升开发效率。目前项目处于早期阶段，主要通过 GitHub 开源社区获取反馈，尚未形成大规模的企业级应用案例。
track_reason: 开源coding agent，供开发者构建agent的基础设施

score_breakdown.reason: 处于 Coding Agent 赛道，具备一定技术创新但商业化与团队背景信息严重不足，属于早期探索性项目。
plus: 具备浏览器环境感知能力
开源社区认可度初步建立
minus: 缺乏商业化路径
创始人背景信息缺失
面临 Cursor 等巨头竞争
技术壁垒尚待验证

full_profile.one_liner: Stagewise — 开源Coding Agent，通过浏览器环境模拟与DOM解析实现代码编辑，强调模型无关性与本地控制，创始人背景信息有限。
full_profile.overview_zh: Stagewise 是一款开源的 Coding Agent，旨在解决传统 Agent 无法直观感知前端 UI 变化的问题。其核心机制是在独立的浏览器环境中运行，通过实时读取 DOM 结构和控制台日志，将视觉反馈与代码库关联，从而实现针对性的代码修改。该工具支持用户接入任意 LLM，强调数据隐私与本地控制。目前主要面向前端开发者，旨在通过“所见即所得”的反馈循环提升开发效率。目前项目处于早期阶段，主要通过 GitHub 开源社区获取反馈，尚未形成大规模的企业级应用案例。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone 视角：Coding Agent 赛道已进入白热化阶段，Stagewise 的差异化在于“浏览器环境感知”，这在处理复杂前端交互时具有一定优势。然而，该领域面临 Cursor 等巨头的强力挤压，单纯的开源工具若无深度的工程化壁垒，极易被集成到大型 IDE 中。Alpha 机会在于其能否在特定复杂前端工作流中实现比 Cursor 更高的成功率。风险在于技术路径的通用性较弱，且缺乏商业化验证，难以在短期内形成护城河。我们的投资论文倾向于寻找具备闭环数据飞轮的 Agent，Stagewise 目前仍偏向于工具属性。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native 得 20 分，因其具备浏览器环境感知能力，属于较好的 Agent 交互设计；Niche 得 13 分，前端感知是痛点，但技术壁垒相对较低；商业模式得 8 分，因开源无明确变现计划；团队得 9 分，因缺乏公开背景信息；加分 4 分，因其在 Product Hunt 获得一定关注且开源属性明确；减分 10 分，因信息严重不足且面临 Cursor 等强力竞品竞争。总分 64 分，处于普通关注区间，需进一步验证其在复杂项目中的稳定性。
full_profile.metrics_summary: Product Hunt: 182票，Featured Rank: 6，GitHub Stars: 暂无公开数据，用户数: 暂无公开数据，ARR: 0

title: Subagents in Gemini CLI
description_en: Gemini CLI's new subagents feature lets the main agent delegate complex tasks to specialised agents, each with isolated context, custom tools, and scoped permissions. For developers building or automating from the terminal.
description_zh: Subagents in Gemini CLI 是 Google 为开发者推出的终端自动化增强功能。其核心机制在于允许主 Agent 将复杂任务拆解并委派给具有独立上下文、特定工具集和受限权限的“子代理（Subagents）”。这种架构旨在解决单一 Agent 在处理长链路任务时上下文丢失或指令冲突的问题。目标用户为深度依赖 CLI 的开发者和 DevOps 工程师，旨在通过终端直接调用 Gemini 模型能力，实现从代码生成到环境部署的自动化闭环。目前该功能已集成在 Google 的开发者工具链中，通过 Product Hunt 获得了一定的社区关注。
track_reason: 为开发者构建Agent提供基础设施，支持Agent编排。

score_breakdown.reason: 作为大厂生态内的功能模块，虽然技术架构合理，但缺乏独立商业化潜力与初创公司的 Alpha 溢价。
plus: 背靠 Google Gemini 强大的模型能力
终端集成符合开发者工作流
任务拆解架构符合 Agent 发展趋势
minus: 属于大厂附属功能，非独立创业项目
商业模式不清晰，仅作为生态引流工具
缺乏跨平台兼容性，被锁定在 Google 生态

full_profile.one_liner: Subagents in Gemini CLI — 基于Google Gemini生态的终端Agent编排工具，通过子代理机制实现复杂任务拆解，由Google官方团队开发。
full_profile.overview_zh: Subagents in Gemini CLI 是 Google 为开发者推出的终端自动化增强功能。其核心机制在于允许主 Agent 将复杂任务拆解并委派给具有独立上下文、特定工具集和受限权限的“子代理（Subagents）”。这种架构旨在解决单一 Agent 在处理长链路任务时上下文丢失或指令冲突的问题。目标用户为深度依赖 CLI 的开发者和 DevOps 工程师，旨在通过终端直接调用 Gemini 模型能力，实现从代码生成到环境部署的自动化闭环。目前该功能已集成在 Google 的开发者工具链中，通过 Product Hunt 获得了一定的社区关注。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前该功能作为 Google Gemini 生态的一部分，主要通过 API 调用量计费（Pay-as-you-go）或集成在 Google Cloud Platform 的订阅体系中。其商业逻辑在于通过降低开发者使用 Agent 的门槛，增加对 Google 模型及云基础设施的粘性。与独立初创公司不同，其商业模式更侧重于生态护城河的构建，而非单一产品的直接变现，因此在独立商业化路径上表现较弱。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone 视角：Agent 编排（Orchestration）是当前开发工具链的必争之地。Gemini CLI 的这一尝试证明了“分治法”在 Agent 架构中的必要性。然而，该项目作为大厂生态内的功能模块，其 Alpha 属性被稀释。市场低估了终端作为 Agent 交互界面的潜力，但高估了单一厂商工具的通用性。风险在于其高度依赖 Google 的闭源生态，且缺乏针对复杂企业级工作流的深度定制能力。我们的投资论文倾向于寻找能够跨模型、跨平台提供统一编排层的中间件，而非单一厂商的附属工具。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native 得 18 分，因为其实现了任务拆解与权限隔离，具备基础的 Agent 编排能力，但仍处于工具调用阶段。Niche 得 12 分，因为终端 Agent 是开发者刚需，但作为 Google 附属功能，缺乏独立壁垒。商业模式得 7 分，因为其本质是生态附庸，缺乏独立的商业闭环。团队得 9 分，背靠 Google 资源，背景扎实但缺乏初创公司的敏捷性。加分项在于其背靠 Google 强大的模型能力与开发者生态，减分项在于其属于大厂产品，缺乏独立创业公司的估值溢价与灵活性，且作为功能模块而非独立产品，其商业价值难以独立评估。
full_profile.metrics_summary: Product Hunt: 142 票，Featured Rank: 9，暂无独立 ARR 数据，属于 Google Cloud/Gemini 生态内功能。

title: Agent Card
description_en: AgentCard gives AI agents their own one-time-use virtual Visa cards. Link your card, let your agent issue a single-use card for each purchase — you're only charged when it actually pays, and cards auto-cancel after one use. Works with Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and any MCP-compatible agent via CLI, MCP, or Chrome Extension.
description_zh: Agent Card 旨在解决 AI Agent 在执行自动化任务（如订阅服务、购买API、电商采购）时的支付痛点。其核心机制是为每个 Agent 任务生成一次性虚拟 Visa 卡，通过 MCP (Model Context Protocol) 或 Chrome 插件集成，确保支付行为在受控范围内。该产品允许用户设置支付限额，卡片在单次使用后自动失效，从而规避了 Agent 权限过大导致的资金风险。目前主要服务于开发者和重度 Agent 用户，通过提供 API 和 CLI 工具，将支付能力嵌入到 Agent 的工作流中。
track_reason: 为AI Agent提供虚拟信用卡，属于开发者基础设施。

score_breakdown.reason: 处于金融基础设施赛道，虽切中痛点但合规壁垒与被大厂降维打击的风险并存，目前仅为早期工具，缺乏深层护城河。
plus: 对 MCP 协议的早期支持
解决了 Agent 自动化支付的安全痛点
产品形态符合 Agent 开发者需求
minus: 金融合规风险极高
缺乏核心技术壁垒，易被 Stripe 等支付巨头整合
团队背景信息缺失
市场规模受限于 Agent 普及度

full_profile.one_liner: Agent Card — 为AI Agent提供一次性虚拟Visa卡支付基础设施，支持MCP协议与主流Agent框架，解决自动化支付安全与授权问题。
full_profile.overview_zh: Agent Card 旨在解决 AI Agent 在执行自动化任务（如订阅服务、购买API、电商采购）时的支付痛点。其核心机制是为每个 Agent 任务生成一次性虚拟 Visa 卡，通过 MCP (Model Context Protocol) 或 Chrome 插件集成，确保支付行为在受控范围内。该产品允许用户设置支付限额，卡片在单次使用后自动失效，从而规避了 Agent 权限过大导致的资金风险。目前主要服务于开发者和重度 Agent 用户，通过提供 API 和 CLI 工具，将支付能力嵌入到 Agent 的工作流中。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 商业模式主要基于支付手续费（Interchange fee）分成或 API 调用订阅。用户通过绑定主卡为 Agent 充值，Agent Card 在每笔交易中抽取一定比例的佣金。其核心价值在于为 Agent 提供“支付权限隔离”，用户愿意付费是因为它降低了自动化支付的欺诈风险和管理成本，相比传统企业卡，其针对 Agent 的细粒度控制是主要差异化点。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone 视角：Agent 支付基础设施是 Agent 经济闭环的关键一环，属于典型的“卖铲子”生意。目前该方向处于早期，随着 Agent 从对话走向执行，支付需求将爆发。然而，该赛道面临极高的合规与金融牌照壁垒（Fintech 监管），且极易被 Stripe 或 Brex 等巨头通过 API 整合降维打击。目前 Agent Card 的壁垒主要在于对 MCP 协议的早期适配，而非金融技术本身。投资论文应关注其能否在合规前提下快速建立开发者生态，而非仅仅作为一个简单的支付工具。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native 得 18 分，因其直接切入 Agent 工作流，但本质仍是支付工具的封装；Niche 得 14 分，支付基础设施是刚需，但护城河较浅；商业模式得 15 分，逻辑清晰且有明确变现路径；团队得 8 分，因缺乏公开背景信息，按默认低分处理；加分 5 分，因其对 MCP 协议的适配体现了对 Agent 趋势的敏锐度；减分 8 分，因金融合规风险极高且缺乏核心技术壁垒，容易被大厂通过 API 整合替代。总分 62 分，属于普通关注，需进一步验证其合规资质与获客成本。
full_profile.metrics_summary: Product Hunt: 114 票，Featured Rank: 13，暂无 ARR 或具体用户数披露。

title: Chinilla
description_en: Like a flight simulator for systems. Drag components, wire them together, hit play, and watch real traffic flow through your system. See queues fill up, databases choke, and bottlenecks form in real time. Scrub a timeline to inspect any moment. Fix what breaks, run it again. That's how you go from knowing patterns to understanding systems. Export to PNG, Mermaid, or Python for your docs and repos. 7 blocks, 12 behaviors, AI that turns code or prompts into live diagrams. Free in your browser.
description_zh: Chinilla 是一款面向系统架构师和后端开发者的可视化仿真工具。它允许用户通过拖拽组件（如队列、数据库、服务）构建系统拓扑，并模拟真实流量流向，实时观察系统在压力下的表现，如队列积压、数据库响应延迟及瓶颈形成。其核心亮点在于“系统飞行模拟器”的概念，支持通过AI将代码或自然语言描述转化为交互式架构图，并能导出为Mermaid或Python代码。该产品旨在解决复杂分布式系统在设计阶段难以预判性能瓶颈的痛点，目前主要作为浏览器端的免费工具提供。
track_reason: 开发者工具，用于构建和调试系统。

score_breakdown.reason: 产品处于早期实验阶段，可视化体验尚可但缺乏深层技术壁垒和商业验证，故给予62分。
plus: 直观的系统瓶颈可视化体验
支持导出Mermaid和Python代码，便于文档化
minus: 缺乏明确的商业化路径
创始人背景信息缺失
仿真精度与生产环境的映射关系存疑
容易被大厂生态工具同质化替代

full_profile.one_liner: Chinilla — 系统架构可视化仿真工具，通过拖拽组件与AI生成模拟流量，帮助开发者直观发现系统瓶颈，创始人背景信息有限。
full_profile.overview_zh: Chinilla 是一款面向系统架构师和后端开发者的可视化仿真工具。它允许用户通过拖拽组件（如队列、数据库、服务）构建系统拓扑，并模拟真实流量流向，实时观察系统在压力下的表现，如队列积压、数据库响应延迟及瓶颈形成。其核心亮点在于“系统飞行模拟器”的概念，支持通过AI将代码或自然语言描述转化为交互式架构图，并能导出为Mermaid或Python代码。该产品旨在解决复杂分布式系统在设计阶段难以预判性能瓶颈的痛点，目前主要作为浏览器端的免费工具提供。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前Chinilla主要作为免费的浏览器端工具提供，尚未披露明确的商业化路径或订阅计划。其潜在的商业模式可能包括：针对企业团队的私有化部署、高级仿真组件库的付费订阅，或作为云基础设施监控平台的辅助工具进行集成。由于缺乏明确的变现机制，目前其商业价值处于探索阶段，难以与成熟的SaaS工具竞争。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：系统架构仿真是一个极具潜力的利基市场，尤其是在微服务架构日益复杂的今天，开发者对“所见即所得”的性能预判需求强烈。Chinilla的Alpha在于将抽象的架构图转化为可执行的仿真环境，降低了系统设计的认知门槛。然而，该方向的风险在于：一是仿真精度难以达到生产环境级别，容易沦为“演示工具”；二是缺乏深度的技术壁垒，容易被云厂商（如AWS/Azure）的内置架构工具覆盖。我们的投资论文倾向于寻找能与实际生产数据打通、具备闭环优化能力的Agent，而Chinilla目前更偏向于设计辅助工具。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native得16分，因为其利用AI生成架构图，但核心仿真逻辑仍依赖预设规则，缺乏自进化能力；Niche得14分，系统仿真领域有需求，但目前功能偏向可视化，护城河较浅；商业模式得8分，因为目前完全免费且无变现计划；团队得9分，因缺乏公开背景信息，按中性处理；减分5分，主要因为创始人背景信息严重不足且产品目前处于早期实验性阶段，缺乏大规模商业验证。
full_profile.metrics_summary: Product Hunt: 107票，Featured Rank: 17，暂无ARR、用户留存及GitHub stars等核心商业数据。

title: Kampala by Zatanna
description_en: Kampala lets you reverse engineer anything including websites, mobile apps, and desktop apps instantly. Kampala takes care of SSO, Auth, and MFA and lets you have complete visibility into what requests trigger what workflows. Complete a workflow once with Kampala open, and it has all the context needed to repeat the workflow including logging in and keeping your cookies warm. Use the MCP or agent harness and run your scripts locally or host an API on our servers with the click of a button.
description_zh: Kampala旨在解决Agent开发中最棘手的“最后一公里”问题：即如何让Agent在复杂的身份验证（SSO、MFA、Cookie管理）环境下稳定运行。该产品通过逆向工程技术，允许开发者在操作一次目标应用（网页、移动端或桌面端）后，自动捕获并封装所有请求上下文。它提供MCP（Model Context Protocol）接口和Agent Harness，支持本地脚本执行或一键云端API部署。其核心价值在于将复杂的交互逻辑转化为可复用的Agent工作流，极大降低了构建自动化Agent的门槛。
track_reason: 提供MCP和Agent框架，供开发者构建Agent。

score_breakdown.reason: 产品切入点精准但处于极早期，社区热度低且存在合规风险，暂不具备高分潜力。
plus: 解决了Agent开发中Auth处理的痛点
支持MCP协议，符合当前Agent生态趋势
minus: Product Hunt票数较低，社区热度不足
逆向工程技术存在合规与安全风险
团队背景信息严重不足
缺乏大规模商业化落地案例

full_profile.one_liner: Kampala by Zatanna — 一个自动化工作流逆向工程与Agent执行平台，通过录制用户行为自动处理SSO/Auth/MFA，由Zatanna团队开发。
full_profile.overview_zh: Kampala旨在解决Agent开发中最棘手的“最后一公里”问题：即如何让Agent在复杂的身份验证（SSO、MFA、Cookie管理）环境下稳定运行。该产品通过逆向工程技术，允许开发者在操作一次目标应用（网页、移动端或桌面端）后，自动捕获并封装所有请求上下文。它提供MCP（Model Context Protocol）接口和Agent Harness，支持本地脚本执行或一键云端API部署。其核心价值在于将复杂的交互逻辑转化为可复用的Agent工作流，极大降低了构建自动化Agent的门槛。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前商业模式尚处于早期阶段，主要通过API调用量或托管服务收费。定价逻辑基于开发者对自动化工作流的依赖程度，通过提供“开箱即用”的Auth处理能力来吸引企业级开发者。与竞品相比，其商业模式更侧重于基础设施层面的“中间件”收费，但目前缺乏明确的订阅增长数据支持，变现路径仍需验证。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：Agent自动化领域存在巨大的“Auth鸿沟”，即大多数Agent在面对企业级SSO和MFA时会失效，Kampala切入点极佳。然而，该方向面临巨大的合规与安全风险，逆向工程技术在企业级应用中可能触及服务条款（ToS）红线。目前市场对该类工具的接受度尚在验证期，且技术壁垒高度依赖于对目标应用更新的响应速度。我们的投资论文关注点在于其能否从单纯的“自动化脚本工具”进化为“企业级Agent身份管理平台”，目前看仍处于早期探索阶段，缺乏足够的护城河。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native得18分，因其具备自动化工作流捕获能力，但尚未形成闭环数据飞轮。Niche得14分，Auth处理是痛点，但技术路径存在合规风险。商业模式得12分，逻辑清晰但缺乏规模化验证。团队得9分，背景信息有限。加减分方面，因Product Hunt票数仅76票，社区认可度不足，且作为早期工具存在信息严重不足的减分项（-3），以及潜在的合规风险减分（-8），总分62分，属于普通关注范畴。
full_profile.metrics_summary: Product Hunt: 76票，Featured Rank: 26，暂无公开ARR或用户数数据。

title: RadAgent: A tool-using AI agent for stepwise interpretation of chest computed tomography
description_en: Vision-language models (VLM) have markedly advanced AI-driven interpretation and reporting of complex medical imaging, such as computed tomography (CT). Yet, existing methods largely relegate clinicians to passive observers of final outputs, offering no interpretable reasoning trace for them to inspect, validate, or refine. To address this, we introduce RadAgent, a tool-using AI agent that generates CT reports through a stepwise and interpretable process. Each resulting report is accompanied by a fully inspectable trace of intermediate decisions and tool interactions, allowing clinicians to examine how the reported findings are derived. In our experiments, we observe that RadAgent improves Chest CT report generation over its 3D VLM counterpart, CT-Chat, across three dimensions. Clinical accuracy improves by 6.0 points (36.4% relative) in macro-F1 and 5.4 points (19.6% relative) in micro-F1. Robustness under adversarial conditions improves by 24.7 points (41.9% relative). Furthermore, RadAgent achieves 37.0% in faithfulness, a new capability entirely absent in its 3D VLM counterpart. By structuring the interpretation of chest CT as an explicit, tool-augmented and iterative reasoning trace, RadAgent brings us closer toward transparent and reliable AI for radiology.
description_zh: RadAgent 旨在解决当前视觉语言模型（VLM）在医学影像诊断中“黑盒”输出的问题。该系统将胸部CT报告生成过程拆解为一系列可追溯的工具调用与推理步骤，而非直接生成最终结论。其核心机制包括：1. 模块化工具调用，允许模型在分析影像时主动检索或验证特定解剖结构；2. 显式推理链，为临床医生提供可审计的决策路径。实验数据显示，在胸部CT报告生成任务中，RadAgent 在宏观F1分数上提升了36.4%，在对抗性条件下的鲁棒性提升了41.9%，并首次引入了37%的忠实度（Faithfulness）指标，显著优于传统的3D VLM模型 CT-Chat。

full_profile.one_liner: RadAgent — 一种用于胸部CT影像解释的工具调用AI Agent，通过分步推理提升诊断可解释性与鲁棒性，由来自ETH Zurich及多家顶级医学AI实验室的研究团队开发。
full_profile.overview_zh: RadAgent 旨在解决当前视觉语言模型（VLM）在医学影像诊断中“黑盒”输出的问题。该系统将胸部CT报告生成过程拆解为一系列可追溯的工具调用与推理步骤，而非直接生成最终结论。其核心机制包括：1. 模块化工具调用，允许模型在分析影像时主动检索或验证特定解剖结构；2. 显式推理链，为临床医生提供可审计的决策路径。实验数据显示，在胸部CT报告生成任务中，RadAgent 在宏观F1分数上提升了36.4%，在对抗性条件下的鲁棒性提升了41.9%，并首次引入了37%的忠实度（Faithfulness）指标，显著优于传统的3D VLM模型 CT-Chat。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前该项目为纯学术研究，暂无明确商业模式。潜在商业路径包括：1. 作为医疗影像诊断软件（PACS）的插件，通过API订阅模式收费；2. 授权给医疗设备厂商（如GE、Siemens）集成至CT扫描仪中；3. 针对医院的合规审计需求提供SaaS服务。由于医疗行业对可解释性（Explainability）有极高的监管要求，该技术具有较强的付费潜力，但目前缺乏落地验证。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：医疗影像AI的下一个范式是从“端到端生成”转向“推理链生成”。RadAgent 的Alpha在于它将AI从单纯的分类器转变为具备临床逻辑的“助手”。市场目前低估了“可解释性”在医疗合规中的价值，这不仅是技术优化，更是AI进入临床工作流的准入证。风险在于：1. 医疗影像数据的获取与标注壁垒极高；2. 临床工作流的集成难度大，医生对AI辅助诊断的信任建立周期长。我们的投资论文倾向于关注那些能将此类推理能力转化为临床决策支持（CDS）系统的团队，而非单纯的论文发表者。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native得18分，因为其设计了显式推理链，具备了Agent的工具调用雏形，但尚未形成闭环工作流。Niche得16分，医疗影像诊断是极高壁垒的垂直领域，但目前仍处于学术验证阶段。商业模式得7分，因为目前完全是学术项目，无变现计划。团队得12分，由ETH Zurich及相关领域专家组成，背景扎实。加分项在于其在鲁棒性和忠实度上的显著提升，这是医疗AI的关键指标；减分项在于缺乏商业落地数据，且创始人背景偏学术，缺乏商业化经验。总分62分，属于值得关注的学术突破，但距离商业化Alpha尚有距离。
full_profile.metrics_summary: 暂无公开数据，仅有论文实验数据：临床准确性宏观F1提升6.0点，鲁棒性提升24.7点，忠实度达到37.0%。

title: Meituan Merchant Business Diagnosis via Policy-Guided Dual-Process User Simulation
description_en: Simulating group-level user behavior enables scalable counterfactual evaluation of merchant strategies without costly online experiments. However, building a trustworthy simulator faces two structural challenges. First, information incompleteness causes reasoning-based simulators to over-rationalize when unobserved factors such as offline context and implicit habits are missing. Second, mechanism duality requires capturing both interpretable preferences and implicit statistical regularities, which no single paradigm achieves alone. We propose Policy-Guided Hybrid Simulation (PGHS), a dual-process framework that mines transferable decision policies from behavioral trajectories and uses them as a shared alignment layer. This layer anchors an LLM-based reasoning branch that prevents over-rationalization and an ML-based fitting branch that absorbs implicit regularities. Group-level predictions from both branches are fused for complementary correction. We deploy PGHS on Meituan with 101 merchants and over 26,000 trajectories. PGHS achieves a group simulation error of 8.80%, improving over the best reasoning-based and fitting-based baselines by 45.8% and 40.9% respectively.
description_zh: PGHS (Policy-Guided Hybrid Simulation) 旨在解决大规模商户策略反事实评估的难题。传统模拟器在面对信息缺失时容易过度理性化，且难以兼顾可解释性与统计规律。该框架通过双过程机制：1. 挖掘可迁移的决策策略作为对齐层；2. 利用LLM分支进行逻辑推理，防止过度理性化；3. 利用ML分支捕捉隐性统计规律。通过在美团101个商户、2.6万条轨迹上的实测，该模型将群体模拟误差降低至8.80%，显著优于单一的推理或拟合基准。

full_profile.one_liner: PGHS — 一种用于美团商户策略评估的策略引导双过程模拟框架，通过融合LLM推理与ML拟合解决用户行为模拟难题，由美团技术团队开发。
full_profile.overview_zh: PGHS (Policy-Guided Hybrid Simulation) 旨在解决大规模商户策略反事实评估的难题。传统模拟器在面对信息缺失时容易过度理性化，且难以兼顾可解释性与统计规律。该框架通过双过程机制：1. 挖掘可迁移的决策策略作为对齐层；2. 利用LLM分支进行逻辑推理，防止过度理性化；3. 利用ML分支捕捉隐性统计规律。通过在美团101个商户、2.6万条轨迹上的实测，该模型将群体模拟误差降低至8.80%，显著优于单一的推理或拟合基准。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 该项目目前为学术研究与内部技术优化，暂无直接商业模式。若转化为产品，其商业逻辑在于通过高精度的反事实模拟，降低商户策略试错成本，提升平台运营效率。潜在收费模式为企业级SaaS订阅或基于策略优化效果的增值服务，通过减少线上A/B测试的流量损耗来体现价值。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：该研究在工业界复杂场景（美团）中验证了混合模拟范式的有效性，这是AI在运筹优化与决策支持领域的重要突破。Alpha在于其解决了LLM在处理非结构化行为数据时的‘过度理性化’偏差，通过双过程架构实现了可解释性与预测精度的平衡。风险在于该模型对特定平台数据的依赖性极强，通用化迁移难度大。我们看好其在复杂电商与本地生活场景下的决策辅助潜力，但需观察其在非美团生态下的泛化能力。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native得16分，因其通过双过程架构实现了模拟闭环，但本质仍是辅助决策工具；Niche得18分，因其精准切入工业界反事实评估的痛点，技术路径有非共识性；商业模式得8分，因目前仅为学术/内部研究，缺乏明确变现路径；团队得12分，美团背景具备极强的领域知识与数据获取优势；加分项在于其在真实工业场景（美团）的验证效果显著，且双过程架构具有较强的学术创新性。总分62分，属于值得关注的技术突破，但距离商业化产品尚有距离。
full_profile.metrics_summary: 美团实测商户数：101；轨迹数据量：26,000+；群体模拟误差：8.80%；较基准提升：45.8% (推理类) / 40.9% (拟合类)。

title: Agent-Aided Design for Dynamic CAD Models
description_en: In the past year, researchers have started to create agentic systems that can design real-world CAD-style objects in a training-free setting, a new variety of system that we call Agent-Aided Design. Generally speaking, these systems place an agent in a feedback loop in which it can write code, compile that code to an assembly of CAD model(s), visualize the model, and then iteratively refine its code based on visual and other feedback. Despite rapid progress, a key problem remains: none of these systems can build complex 3D assemblies with moving parts. For example, no existing system can build a piston, a pendulum, or even a pair of scissors. In order for Agent-Aided Design to make a real impact in industrial manufacturing, we need a system that is capable of generating such 3D assemblies. In this paper we present a prototype of AADvark, an agentic system designed for this task. Unlike previous state-of-the-art systems, AADvark captures the dynamic part interactions with one or more degrees-of-freedom. This design decision allows AADvark to reason directly about assemblies with moving parts and can thereby achieve cross-cutting goals, including but not limited to mechanical movements. Unfortunately, current LLMs are imperfect spatial reasoners, a problem that AADvark addresses by incorporating external constraint solver tools with a specialized visual feedback mechanism. We demonstrate that, by modifying the agent's tools (FreeCAD and the assembly solver), we are able to create a strong verification signal which enables our system to build 3D assemblies with movable parts.
description_zh: AADvark旨在解决当前Agent辅助设计（Agent-Aided Design）在处理复杂动态机械装配时的局限性。现有系统多局限于静态几何体生成，而AADvark通过引入对动态部件交互和自由度（DoF）的显式建模，使Agent能够通过代码编写、编译、可视化及反馈循环，生成如活塞、摆锤等具备运动功能的3D装配体。该系统通过将物理运动逻辑嵌入Agent的反馈回路，填补了AI在工业制造领域从静态建模向动态机械设计跨越的技术空白。

full_profile.one_liner: AADvark — 针对动态CAD模型设计的Agent系统，通过引入自由度(DoF)推理实现复杂机械装配，由Michael Cafarella等学者开发。
full_profile.overview_zh: AADvark旨在解决当前Agent辅助设计（Agent-Aided Design）在处理复杂动态机械装配时的局限性。现有系统多局限于静态几何体生成，而AADvark通过引入对动态部件交互和自由度（DoF）的显式建模，使Agent能够通过代码编写、编译、可视化及反馈循环，生成如活塞、摆锤等具备运动功能的3D装配体。该系统通过将物理运动逻辑嵌入Agent的反馈回路，填补了AI在工业制造领域从静态建模向动态机械设计跨越的技术空白。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前该项目为纯学术研究原型，暂无商业化计划。若未来落地，潜在模式为向工业设计软件（如SolidWorks, Autodesk）提供插件或API授权，通过按需生成复杂机械部件的算力消耗或订阅费获取收益。其核心价值在于大幅缩短机械工程师在原型设计阶段的迭代周期。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：该方向具有极高的Alpha潜力，因为工业CAD设计是AI Agent从“数字内容生成”跨越到“物理世界制造”的关键节点。目前市场过度关注LLM在代码和文本上的表现，而忽略了将物理约束（如运动学、自由度）融入Agent推理链的深层技术壁垒。风险在于CAD软件生态的封闭性以及生成代码的鲁棒性，若无法与主流工业软件无缝集成，其商业化路径将极其漫长。我们关注其能否在特定垂直领域（如机器人零部件设计）形成闭环。

title: MM-WebAgent: A Hierarchical Multimodal Web Agent for Webpage Generation
description_en: The rapid progress of Artificial Intelligence Generated Content (AIGC) tools enables images, videos, and visualizations to be created on demand for webpage design, offering a flexible and increasingly adopted paradigm for modern UI/UX. However, directly integrating such tools into automated webpage generation often leads to style inconsistency and poor global coherence, as elements are generated in isolation. We propose MM-WebAgent, a hierarchical agentic framework for multimodal webpage generation that coordinates AIGC-based element generation through hierarchical planning and iterative self-reflection. MM-WebAgent jointly optimizes global layout, local multimodal content, and their integration, producing coherent and visually consistent webpages. We further introduce a benchmark for multimodal webpage generation and a multi-level evaluation protocol for systematic assessment. Experiments demonstrate that MM-WebAgent outperforms code-generation and agent-based baselines, especially on multimodal element generation and integration. Code & Data: https://aka.ms/mm-webagent.
description_zh: MM-WebAgent 旨在解决当前AIGC工具在网页设计中生成的元素孤立、风格不统一及全局连贯性差的问题。该框架采用层级化Agent架构，将网页生成任务分解为全局布局规划、局部多模态内容生成及最终的集成优化三个阶段。通过引入迭代式自反射机制，Agent能够实时评估并修正生成的视觉元素与代码结构，确保网页在视觉风格和功能逻辑上的高度一致性。该项目还配套发布了多模态网页生成基准测试集及多层级评估协议，为自动化UI/UX设计提供了系统化的技术验证路径。
track_reason: arXiv论文，主要价值是技术突破本身。

score_breakdown.reason: 作为纯学术技术突破，其在Agent架构设计上有Alpha，但因缺乏商业化落地数据和产品化验证，故给予62分，处于普通关注区间。

full_profile.one_liner: MM-WebAgent — 一种用于多模态网页生成的层级化Agent框架，通过层级规划与自反射机制解决AIGC生成的全局一致性问题，由Microsoft团队开发。
full_profile.overview_zh: MM-WebAgent 旨在解决当前AIGC工具在网页设计中生成的元素孤立、风格不统一及全局连贯性差的问题。该框架采用层级化Agent架构，将网页生成任务分解为全局布局规划、局部多模态内容生成及最终的集成优化三个阶段。通过引入迭代式自反射机制，Agent能够实时评估并修正生成的视觉元素与代码结构，确保网页在视觉风格和功能逻辑上的高度一致性。该项目还配套发布了多模态网页生成基准测试集及多层级评估协议，为自动化UI/UX设计提供了系统化的技术验证路径。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前该项目为学术研究性质，暂无商业化变现计划。若转化为商业产品，其逻辑应为：通过API订阅或SaaS平台向UI/UX设计师、前端开发团队提供自动化网页生成服务。核心价值在于大幅缩短从设计草图到代码实现的周期，并降低多模态素材整合的门槛。其商业壁垒在于对复杂UI布局的语义理解能力及与现有设计工具链（如Figma）的集成深度。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：网页生成是Agent应用中极具挑战的领域，因为其不仅要求视觉美感，还要求代码的严谨性。MM-WebAgent的Alpha在于其‘层级化规划+自反射’的范式，这比单纯的端到端生成更具可控性。市场目前低估了‘多模态一致性’在企业级网页自动化中的价值，大多数工具仍停留在简单的代码生成。风险在于该技术在复杂动态交互场景下的鲁棒性，以及如何处理企业级设计系统的约束。我们的投资论文将关注该技术能否从学术基准走向实际的生产力工具，特别是其在低代码平台中的集成潜力。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native得18分，因其引入了层级规划与自反射，具备了Agent闭环雏形；Niche得16分，多模态网页生成是高门槛领域，但目前仍处于学术验证阶段；商业模式得7分，因纯学术项目无变现计划；团队得12分，由Microsoft团队主导，背景扎实；加分项得9分，因其提供了开源基准测试集（+2）、顶级大厂背景（+3）、以及在Agentic Workflow方向的明确技术贡献（+4）。总分62分，反映了其作为前沿技术研究的潜力，但距离商业化落地仍有较大距离。
full_profile.metrics_summary: 暂无公开数据，仅提供arXiv论文及GitHub代码库链接。

title: RAD-2: Scaling Reinforcement Learning in a Generator-Discriminator Framework
description_en: High-level autonomous driving requires motion planners capable of modeling multimodal future uncertainties while remaining robust in closed-loop interactions. Although diffusion-based planners are effective at modeling complex trajectory distributions, they often suffer from stochastic instabilities and the lack of corrective negative feedback when trained purely with imitation learning. To address these issues, we propose RAD-2, a unified generator-discriminator framework for closed-loop planning. Specifically, a diffusion-based generator is used to produce diverse trajectory candidates, while an RL-optimized discriminator reranks these candidates according to their long-term driving quality. This decoupled design avoids directly applying sparse scalar rewards to the full high-dimensional trajectory space, thereby improving optimization stability. To further enhance reinforcement learning, we introduce Temporally Consistent Group Relative Policy Optimization, which exploits temporal coherence to alleviate the credit assignment problem. In addition, we propose On-policy Generator Optimization, which converts closed-loop feedback into structured longitudinal optimization signals and progressively shifts the generator toward high-reward trajectory manifolds. To support efficient large-scale training, we introduce BEV-Warp, a high-throughput simulation environment that performs closed-loop evaluation directly in Bird's-Eye View feature space via spatial warping. RAD-2 reduces the collision rate by 56% compared with strong diffusion-based planners. Real-world deployment further demonstrates improved perceived safety and driving smoothness in complex urban traffic.
description_zh: RAD-2 旨在解决自动驾驶中运动规划的鲁棒性与多模态不确定性问题。传统扩散模型在模仿学习下缺乏负反馈，导致闭环不稳。RAD-2 采用生成器-判别器架构：扩散模型生成轨迹候选集，RL优化的判别器进行重排序。核心创新包括：1. 引入“时间一致性组相对策略优化”（Temporally Consistent Group Relative Policy Optimization）解决信用分配难题；2. “在线生成器优化”（On-policy Generator Optimization）将闭环反馈转化为结构化信号。该方法有效提升了复杂交互场景下的规划稳定性与安全性。
track_reason: 纯技术论文，主要价值是技术突破本身。

score_breakdown.reason: 作为纯学术突破，其技术价值极高，但缺乏商业化验证，故给予62分，属于值得关注的底层技术Alpha。
plus: 解决了扩散模型在自动驾驶中的不稳定性
引入了创新的时间一致性策略优化
架构设计具有较强的学术前瞻性
minus: 缺乏商业化落地数据

full_profile.one_liner: RAD-2 — 一种用于自动驾驶的生成式判别框架，通过RL优化扩散模型轨迹规划，由华中科技大学Xinggang Wang团队提出。
full_profile.overview_zh: RAD-2 旨在解决自动驾驶中运动规划的鲁棒性与多模态不确定性问题。传统扩散模型在模仿学习下缺乏负反馈，导致闭环不稳。RAD-2 采用生成器-判别器架构：扩散模型生成轨迹候选集，RL优化的判别器进行重排序。核心创新包括：1. 引入“时间一致性组相对策略优化”（Temporally Consistent Group Relative Policy Optimization）解决信用分配难题；2. “在线生成器优化”（On-policy Generator Optimization）将闭环反馈转化为结构化信号。该方法有效提升了复杂交互场景下的规划稳定性与安全性。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 作为纯学术研究项目，目前无直接商业模式。其商业潜力在于通过授权专利或技术转让给自动驾驶Tier 1供应商或主机厂（OEM），通过提升规划算法的安全性与长尾场景处理能力，降低自动驾驶系统的研发与测试成本。由于是底层算法优化，其价值在于作为自动驾驶软件栈的核心组件，而非独立SaaS产品。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：自动驾驶规划正从单纯的模仿学习向RL强化学习范式迁移，RAD-2 提出的生成器-判别器解耦设计是解决RL在复杂高维空间训练不稳定的关键路径。市场目前低估了“闭环反馈”在生成式规划中的重要性。风险在于该算法的推理延迟（Latency）是否满足车规级实时性要求，以及在真实路测中的泛化能力。我们的投资论文关注那些能将此类前沿算法工程化并落地于量产车型的技术团队，RAD-2 提供了极佳的算法基准。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native得18分，因为其设计逻辑完全围绕闭环反馈与生成式规划，属于自动驾驶领域的深度AI重构。Niche得19分，自动驾驶规划是极高壁垒的垂直领域，该技术路径具有较强的非共识性。商业模式得5分，因其为纯学术论文，缺乏商业化变现路径。团队得12分，由华中科技大学Xinggang Wang教授团队领衔，在计算机视觉与自动驾驶领域有深厚积累。加分项得8分，主要因为该论文在解决扩散模型在自动驾驶中“幻觉”与“不稳”问题上具有极高的技术参考价值，且属于当前自动驾驶领域最前沿的RL+Diffusion结合方向。
full_profile.metrics_summary: 暂无公开数据，仅为Arxiv预印本论文。

title: Think in Latent Thoughts: A New Paradigm for Gloss-Free Sign Language Translation
description_en: Many SLT systems quietly assume that brief chunks of signing map directly to spoken-language words. That assumption breaks down because signers often create meaning on the fly using context, space, and movement. We revisit SLT and argue that it is mainly a cross-modal reasoning task, not just a straightforward video-to-text conversion. We thus introduce a reasoning-driven SLT framework that uses an ordered sequence of latent thoughts as an explicit middle layer between the video and the generated text. These latent thoughts gradually extract and organize meaning over time. On top of this, we use a plan-then-ground decoding method: the model first decides what it wants to say, and then looks back at the video to find the evidence. This separation improves coherence and faithfulness. We also built and released a new large-scale gloss-free SLT dataset with stronger context dependencies and more realistic meanings. Experiments across several benchmarks show consistent gains over existing gloss-free methods. Code and data will be released upon acceptance at https://github.com/fletcherjiang/SignThought.
description_zh: 该项目针对传统手语翻译（SLT）系统过度依赖词汇标注（Gloss）且忽略手语时空上下文的问题，提出了一种全新的推理驱动框架。其核心机制在于引入“潜在思维（Latent Thoughts）”作为视频到文本的中间层，通过“先规划（Plan）后对齐（Ground）”的解码策略，使模型在生成文本前先构建语义逻辑，再从视频中提取证据。这种方法显著提升了翻译的连贯性和忠实度。团队还发布了一个大规模、无词汇标注的SLT数据集，旨在解决手语翻译中复杂的上下文依赖问题，实验结果显示其在多个基准测试中优于现有方法。
track_reason: arXiv论文，主要价值是技术突破本身。

score_breakdown.reason: 技术创新点明确，但商业化路径极度模糊，且缺乏实际应用数据支撑，属于典型的早期学术探索。
plus: 提出了创新的潜在思维中间层架构
发布了大规模无词汇标注数据集
解决了手语翻译中常见的上下文丢失问题
minus: 目前仅为学术论文，缺乏商业落地验证

full_profile.overview_zh: 该项目针对传统手语翻译（SLT）系统过度依赖词汇标注（Gloss）且忽略手语时空上下文的问题，提出了一种全新的推理驱动框架。其核心机制在于引入“潜在思维（Latent Thoughts）”作为视频到文本的中间层，通过“先规划（Plan）后对齐（Ground）”的解码策略，使模型在生成文本前先构建语义逻辑，再从视频中提取证据。这种方法显著提升了翻译的连贯性和忠实度。团队还发布了一个大规模、无词汇标注的SLT数据集，旨在解决手语翻译中复杂的上下文依赖问题，实验结果显示其在多个基准测试中优于现有方法。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前该项目为纯学术研究，暂无商业化计划。潜在商业模式包括：1. 为辅助技术公司提供API接口，实现实时手语转文字服务；2. 授权数据集给大模型厂商进行多模态预训练；3. 针对医疗、公共服务领域提供定制化翻译引擎。由于目前处于学术验证阶段，商业逻辑尚不清晰，主要依赖于技术领先性带来的潜在授权价值。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：手语翻译长期受限于数据标注成本高昂和跨模态语义鸿沟，该研究通过引入思维链（CoT）思想解决手语的时空推理问题，具有较强的技术Alpha。市场被低估之处在于，手语翻译不仅是辅助工具，更是多模态大模型理解复杂动态视觉语义的极佳测试场。风险在于该技术距离工业级实时部署仍有距离，且手语的方言化和非标准化特征极难通过单一模型解决。我们的投资论文将关注该技术在通用视频理解模型中的迁移潜力，而非单纯的手语翻译市场。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native得18分，因为其引入了显式的推理中间层，重构了从视觉到语言的映射逻辑；Niche得19分，因为手语翻译是多模态领域极具挑战性的长尾场景，技术壁垒较高；商业模式得7分，因目前仅为学术项目，变现路径缺失；团队得10分，背景合理但缺乏产业化经验；加分4分，因其开源数据集和代码对社区有贡献；减分6分，因缺乏真实商业数据且目前仅为arXiv论文，距离落地有较大不确定性。总分62分，处于普通关注范畴，需观察其在工业界复现的效果。
full_profile.metrics_summary: 暂无公开数据，GitHub仓库尚未正式发布，仅有arXiv论文发表记录。

title: SegWithU: Uncertainty as Perturbation Energy for Single-Forward-Pass Risk-Aware Medical Image Segmentation
description_en: Reliable uncertainty estimation is critical for medical image segmentation, where automated contours feed downstream quantification and clinical decision support. Many strong uncertainty methods require repeated inference, while efficient single-forward-pass alternatives often provide weaker failure ranking or rely on restrictive feature-space assumptions. We present $\textbf{SegWithU}$, a post-hoc framework that augments a frozen pretrained segmentation backbone with a lightweight uncertainty head. SegWithU taps intermediate backbone features and models uncertainty as perturbation energy in a compact probe space using rank-1 posterior probes. It produces two voxel-wise uncertainty maps: a calibration-oriented map for probability tempering and a ranking-oriented map for error detection and selective prediction. Across ACDC, BraTS2024, and LiTS, SegWithU is the strongest and most consistent single-forward-pass baseline, achieving AUROC/AURC of $0.9838/2.4885$, $0.9946/0.2660$, and $0.9925/0.8193$, respectively, while preserving segmentation quality. These results suggest that perturbation-based uncertainty modeling is an effective and practical route to reliability-aware medical segmentation.   Source code is available at https://github.com/ProjectNeura/SegWithU.
description_zh: SegWithU 旨在解决医疗影像分割中不确定性估计的可靠性问题。传统方法通常依赖多次推理（如蒙特卡洛采样），计算成本高昂。SegWithU 提出了一种后置框架，通过在冻结的预训练分割骨干网络上增加一个轻量级不确定性头，利用中间特征将不确定性建模为紧凑探针空间中的“扰动能量”。该方法能生成校准导向和排序导向两类体素级不确定性图，在 ACDC、BraTS2024 和 LiTS 数据集上表现出极高的错误检测能力（AUROC/AURC 指标优异），且无需改变原始分割模型的架构，实现了效率与精度的平衡。

full_profile.overview_zh: SegWithU 旨在解决医疗影像分割中不确定性估计的可靠性问题。传统方法通常依赖多次推理（如蒙特卡洛采样），计算成本高昂。SegWithU 提出了一种后置框架，通过在冻结的预训练分割骨干网络上增加一个轻量级不确定性头，利用中间特征将不确定性建模为紧凑探针空间中的“扰动能量”。该方法能生成校准导向和排序导向两类体素级不确定性图，在 ACDC、BraTS2024 和 LiTS 数据集上表现出极高的错误检测能力（AUROC/AURC 指标优异），且无需改变原始分割模型的架构，实现了效率与精度的平衡。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 该项目目前为纯学术研究，暂无明确商业模式。若转化为商业产品，其潜在模式包括：作为医疗影像诊断软件（SaMD）的合规性增强模块，通过API授权给医疗器械厂商，或作为临床决策支持系统（CDSS）的风险预警插件。用户付费逻辑在于降低医疗误诊风险带来的法律责任与临床成本，但目前缺乏具体的定价策略与市场化路径。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：医疗影像的“可解释性”与“风险感知”是AI进入临床的核心门槛。SegWithU 的 Alpha 在于其将不确定性转化为“扰动能量”的数学建模，这比传统的贝叶斯近似更具计算效率。然而，该方向的风险在于学术界与临床落地之间存在巨大的合规鸿沟。我们认为该技术若能集成进主流影像工作流（如PACS系统），将具有极高的临床价值。投资论文应关注其能否从学术论文转化为可嵌入的工业级SDK，并解决医疗监管中的黑盒问题。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native得16分，因其属于医疗影像分析的辅助工具，非Agent架构；Niche得18分，医疗影像不确定性估计是高壁垒且极具临床价值的细分领域；商业模式得7分，目前仅为学术论文，无变现计划；团队得10分，背景为学术研究人员，缺乏商业化履历；加分项在于其在多个基准数据集上的卓越性能（+4）；减分项在于缺乏商业化路径（-4）及信息不足（-3）。总分62分，属于需要持续关注的学术突破，但目前尚不具备直接投资的Alpha条件。

description_zh: openai/openai-agents-python 是 OpenAI 官方发布的用于构建多智能体工作流的 Python 框架。该框架旨在解决当前 Agent 开发中碎片化的问题，通过提供标准化的接口，使开发者能够更轻松地定义 Agent 行为、管理状态以及处理复杂的工具调用。其核心机制在于深度集成 OpenAI 的模型能力，通过轻量级的抽象层，让开发者专注于业务逻辑而非底层通信协议。目前该项目处于早期阶段，主要面向希望在生产环境中部署可靠 Agent 的开发者群体，旨在成为 Agent 开发的事实标准。
